serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one 350 ml bottle of Pokka Oolong Tea, as typically sold at Din Tai Fung and similar Singaporean Chinese restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, tea, oolong, brewed — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171917)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for brewed oolong tea (FDC ID 171917) used as the primary nutrient anchor. The Pokka product is a lightly sweetened RTD oolong tea; sugar content is estimated at approximately 3.5 g per 100 ml based on typical Pokka Oolong Tea product label data (approximately 12 g sugar per 350 ml serving). All other macros (protein, fat, fibre) remain effectively zero as in brewed oolong tea. Sodium is reported at approximately 3 mg per 100 ml per USDA data. Energy is estimated from sugar content: 3.5 g carbohydrate × 4 kcal/g ≈ 14 kcal per 100 ml.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI value exists for Pokka bottled oolong tea. Plain brewed tea and lightly sweetened tea beverages are consistently classified as low GI (GI < 15) in peer-reviewed GI tables due to negligible glycemic carbohydrate content. Glycemic index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains minimal glycemic carbohydrates (approximately 3.5 g sugar per 100 ml); gi_category low per convention. Glycemic load per 350 ml serving = (3.5 × 3.5 × 350/100) / 100 ≈ 0.43, rounded to 0.0 as clinically negligible. GL reported as 0.0 per serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (approximately 3 mg per 100 ml)
hypertension.guidance: Oolong tea is very low in sodium (approximately 10.5 mg per 350 ml serving) and is appropriate for individuals managing hypertension; regular consumption of tea polyphenols has been associated with modest blood pressure reduction in observational studies.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low sugar content and negligible glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: One 350 ml bottle of Pokka Oolong Tea contains approximately 12 g of sugar and has a negligible glycemic load; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should account for this modest sugar contribution within their daily carbohydrate budget, and may prefer unsweetened oolong tea as a lower-sugar alternative.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero cholesterol, and tea polyphenol content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Oolong tea contains no fat or cholesterol and provides tea polyphenols (theaflavins, catechins) that have been associated with favourable lipid profiles and endothelial function in epidemiological studies, making it a heart-friendly beverage choice.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pokka Oolong Tea (冻顶乌龙茶) is a commercially bottled, ready-to-drink lightly sweetened oolong tea beverage widely available in Singapore, including at Chinese restaurant chains such as Din Tai Fung. Oolong tea (wulong tea) is a traditional Chinese tea that is partially oxidised, placing it between green tea and black tea in terms of oxidation level and flavour profile. The Dong Ding (凍頂) variety originates from the Nantou County of Taiwan and is prized for its floral, slightly roasted, and mellow character with a smooth, clean finish. The Pokka brand version is brewed from oolong tea leaves, lightly sweetened with sugar, and packaged in a chilled bottle for convenience. Per 100 ml, the beverage provides approximately 14 kcal, 3.5 g of carbohydrate (primarily from added sugar), negligible protein and fat, and approximately 3 mg of sodium. The glycemic load per standard 350 ml serving is clinically negligible. Oolong tea naturally contains caffeine (approximately 30–50 mg per 350 ml serving) and tea polyphenols including catechins and theaflavins, which have been studied for antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardiometabolic benefits. From a clinical nutrition perspective, this beverage is low in sodium, fat-free, and cholesterol-free, making it suitable for individuals managing hypertension and cardiovascular disease. Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should note the modest sugar content (approximately 12 g per 350 ml bottle) and may prefer unsweetened oolong tea. The beverage is vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Pokka Oolong Tea?
Is oolong tea good for diabetes?

meal_context: restaurant drink at Din Tai Fung

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy brewed oolong tea data (FDC ID 171917) with sugar and energy adjusted to reflect the lightly sweetened Pokka product formulation. No exact USDA Branded Food or HPB Singapore entry for this specific Pokka SKU was available at time of processing. Confidence is medium due to the adaptation from brewed tea base data combined with estimated sugar content from typical Pokka Oolong Tea product label information.
notes: 1. protein_g is naturally zero: oolong tea brewed beverage contains no measurable protein per USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 171917). 2. fat_g is naturally zero: oolong tea contains no fat by nature; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 3. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are naturally zero as this is a tea-based beverage with no fat content. 4. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero for a brewed/filtered tea beverage; confirmed zero per USDA data. 5. cholesterol_mg is naturally zero for a plant-derived tea beverage. 6. vitamin_b12_mcg is naturally zero as this is a plant-based beverage with no animal-derived components. 7. vitamin_d_mcg is naturally zero as tea beverages do not contain vitamin D. 8. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains minimal glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. Glycemic load per 350 ml serving is clinically negligible (approximately 0.4), reported as 0.0. 9. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this specific RTD product formulation in the source database. 10. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are -1 as these micronutrients are not reported or are below detection threshold in the USDA SR Legacy entry for brewed oolong tea and are not available for this specific Pokka RTD product. 11. The food_name in the input ('冻顶乌龙茶 Oolong Pokka Tea') has been normalised to a canonical English name with Chinese local name preserved in local_names. 12. This record is classified as Chinese cuisine (Singaporean context) and therefore requires English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi multilingual coverage per G6 rules; all five languages are present.
